cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. So the story is about Mr. Williams who was driving and came across a crossroads with a "SLOW" sign. He slowed down, checked both ways, and then drove across without stopping completely. Then a policeman stopped him, saying he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams explained that the sign said "SLOW," not "STOP," and he did slow down. The policeman then realized he was in the wrong street. The question asks what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The options are: A) was driving at a high speed B) was driving onto the main road C) was going to stop his car D) was driving slowly Let me go through the story again to make sure I get the details right. The key points here are the sign (SLOW, not STOP), Mr. Williams slowing down, looking both ways, and then driving across without stopping. The policeman accuses him of not stopping, but Mr. Williams says he slowed down because the sign said SLOW. The policeman then realizes he was in the wrong street, implying maybe he was supposed to be on a different road where the sign was different? So, when the policeman stopped Mr. Williams, what was he doing? The story says that Mr. Williams had already slowed down and was driving across the main road. The policeman approached him after he had done that. The question is about the moment he was stopped. The options are about his actions at that time. Option D says he was driving slowly. Since he had slowed down as per the sign, and he didn't stop, he would have been driving slowly when the policeman stopped him. The policeman's point was that he didn't stop, but Mr. Williams did slow down. Therefore, at the time of being stopped, he was driving slowly, not at high speed. So option D makes sense. Option B says he was driving onto the main road. The story says he drove across without stopping. If he was on the main road, he wouldn't need to stop or slow down, but the sign was SLOW, so he was on a secondary road approaching the main road. So he was driving onto the main road.
